title: sorghum bicolor var. miliiforme
intro: Sorghum bicolor var. miliiforme, also known as broom sorghum, is a variety of sorghum that is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. The medicinal properties of broom sorghum are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which provide health benefits including antioxidant, anti-diabetic, and cardiovascular protective effects. Broom sorghum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in traditional remedies in the form of poultices, ointments, and tinctures.
